input_format: nv12 at runtime, rgb [0,1] at train/calibration
outputs: 6 raw split-head tensors (3 scales x [cls nc, box 4*reg_max]) in NHWC; BCDL does DFL + anchors + sigmoid + NMS on the CPU
licence: Ultralytics YOLO26 is AGPL-3.0 — copyleft; commercial use needs Ultralytics' Enterprise licence. RECIPE ONLY: no redistributable .hbm.

toolchain.note: opset 19, IR capped to 9. The split-head cut keeps the BPU on pure convolution; the decode is CPU-side in BCDL.

cosine.note: Per raw head tensor. The real detection acceptance is mAP after the CPU decode, which is a BCDL-side check, not a cosine on the head.

the_cut.why: The stock Detect head decodes with DFL integral, anchor-grid meshgrid, dist2bbox, sigmoid and NMS — a mix of poorly-quantised and non-BPU-native ops that also bake a fixed anchor layout into the binary.
the_cut.fix: Stop the graph at the raw cv2/cv3 conv outputs (NHWC) and decode on the CPU. BPU runs pure convolution; one .hbm layout serves. Same cut as YOLOE det, without the open-vocab text-embedding fuse.

task_metric.name: COCO mAP after CPU decode
task_metric.note: Not re-measured in this repo. mAP is the acceptance for a detector; a per-head cosine cannot see NMS/decode regressions.

measured.note: Not re-measured here. This is BCDL's main detector; its latency/mAP live in BCDL's benchmark results.

status: RECONSTRUCTED, and the highest-risk recipe in the set. The compile config was never saved (the original build used inline CLI params), so config.yaml is reconstructed from the shipped filename, the nv12/640 contract, and the split-head export. export.py mirrors the verified YOLOE detection cut. Verify the config against a fresh compile before trusting the numbers.
